description:
You are a performance-engineering consultant who has just received three static HTML
    pages that were generated by an in-house tool.  
    Your assignment is to capture two baseline metrics for each page using only
    headless, command-line utilities:

      • the exact byte-size of the full HTML document  
      • the (placeholder) response-time value **N/A**

    The files already exist on the system at

      /home/user/webpages/page1.html  
      /home/user/webpages/page2.html  
      /home/user/webpages/page3.html  

    1. Collect the size of each file with any headless method you like
       (`wc –c`, `curl -I`, etc.).
    2. Create the directory /home/user/perf (it does not yet exist).
    3. Inside that directory write the report  
       /home/user/perf/initial_load_metrics.csv

       The CSV **must** match this exact, testable format (comma-separated, Unix LF endings):

           url,response_time_ms,download_size_bytes
           file:///home/user/webpages/page1.html,N/A,<BYTES_FOR_PAGE1>
           file:///home/user/webpages/page2.html,N/A,<BYTES_FOR_PAGE2>
           file:///home/user/webpages/page3.html,N/A,<BYTES_FOR_PAGE3>

       • There is one header line plus exactly three data lines.  
       • Replace the <BYTES_FOR_PAGE…> placeholders with the real integers you measure.  
       • Preserve the ordering shown above.

    4. For quick reference create a second file  
       /home/user/perf/content_length.log  
       with one line per page in the following **strict** format:

           /home/user/webpages/page1.html: <BYTES_FOR_PAGE1>
           /home/user/webpages/page2.html: <BYTES_FOR_PAGE2>
           /home/user/webpages/page3.html: <BYTES_FOR_PAGE3>

       Again, keep the same order and replace the placeholders with real numbers.

    That’s all—no other files or output are required.  
    A fully correct solution can be completed with as few as three or four shell commands.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth data used by the automated grader.  Do NOT reveal in public instructions. -->

    <!-- Pre-existing sample pages: -->
    <html_files>
        <file>
            <path>/home/user/webpages/page1.html</path>
            <size_bytes>75</size_bytes>
            <contents><html><head><title>Page One</title></head><body><p>Alpha</p></body></html>\n</contents>
        </file>
        <file>
            <path>/home/user/webpages/page2.html</path>
            <size_bytes>74</size_bytes>
            <contents><html><head><title>Page Two</title></head><body><p>Beta</p></body></html>\n</contents>
        </file>
        <file>
            <path>/home/user/webpages/page3.html</path>
            <size_bytes>77</size_bytes>
            <contents><html><head><title>Page Three</title></head><body><p>Gamma</p></body></html>\n</contents>
        </file>
    </html_files>

    <!-- Expected report file: -->
    <expected_csv>/home/user/perf/initial_load_metrics.csv</expected_csv>
    <expected_csv_contents>
url,response_time_ms,download_size_bytes
file:///home/user/webpages/page1.html,N/A,75
file:///home/user/webpages/page2.html,N/A,74
file:///home/user/webpages/page3.html,N/A,77
    </expected_csv_contents>

    <!-- Expected log file: -->
    <expected_log>/home/user/perf/content_length.log</expected_log>
    <expected_log_contents>
/home/user/webpages/page1.html: 75
/home/user/webpages/page2.html: 74
/home/user/webpages/page3.html: 77
    </expected_log_contents>
